# [MLIR] Segmentation fault in AliasOperationPrinter when processing gpu program

**Author:** jiayiw2008
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/182747
**Status:** Open
**Labels:** obsolete, mlir, crash, generated by fuzzer

## Body

Hi, I found a segmentation fault in the following GPU kernel program.

#### Code Example
```llvm
module {
  func.func @kernelAddConstant(%arg0: memref<?xi32>, %arg1: i32) attributes {llvm.emit_c_interface} {
    %0 = gpu.global_id  x
    %c10 = arith.constant 10 : index
    %cond = arith.cmpi slt, %0, %c10 : index
    scf.while (%iter = %1) : (i32) -> i32 {
      %1 = memref.load %arg0[%0] : memref<?xi32>
      %2 = arith.addi %iter, %arg1 : i32
      scf.condition(%cond) %2 : i32
    } do {
    ^bb0(%arg2: i32):
      memref.store %arg2, %arg0[%0] : memref<?xi32>
      scf.yield
    }
    gpu.terminator
  }
}
```

#### Crash Log
```bash
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace and instructions to reproduce the bug.
Stack dump:
```

### Comment 1 - llvmbot


@llvm/issue-subscribers-mlir

Author: PureLove77 (jiayiw2008)

<details>
Hi, I found a segmentation fault in the following GPU kernel program.

#### Code Example
```llvm
module {
  func.func @<!-- -->kernelAddConstant(%arg0: memref&lt;?xi32&gt;, %arg1: i32) attributes {llvm.emit_c_interface} {
    %0 = gpu.global_id  x
    %c10 = arith.constant 10 : index
    %cond = arith.cmpi slt, %0, %c10 : index
    scf.while (%iter = %1) : (i32) -&gt; i32 {
      %1 = memref.load %arg0[%0] : memref&lt;?xi32&gt;
      %2 = arith.addi %iter, %arg1 : i32
      scf.condition(%cond) %2 : i32
    } do {
    ^bb0(%arg2: i32):
      memref.store %arg2, %arg0[%0] : memref&lt;?xi32&gt;
      scf.yield
    }
    gpu.terminator
  }
}
```

### Comment 2 - joker-eph

This does not seem to reproduce at HEAD, can you try an reopen if it is the case?

In general: please provide the git hash when you file bugs.

---

### Comment 3 - jiayiw2008

Hi @joker-eph , my MLIR version is 7dbfc2bd0078b36e4f9ed14dd25c4d8e97b3ae77. I'm encountering a compilation error when building the HEAD branch, related to conflicts between CUDA runtime and PCH. After resolving these issues, I'll try to reproduce it.



---

### Comment 4 - jiayiw2008

I used the version bded7e9e49e5cc4cbb73c54ac7b113a006f0534c, and this issue has been fixed. Thanks!

---

### Comment 5 - jiayiw2008
